No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on 'social responsibility' and 'problem-solving skills' could indirectly apply to creating solutions for poverty, though it's not an explicit focus.
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)
問題解決能力(Students will acquire problem solving skills.)

Zero Hunger.reason: There is no explicit or inferable content in the course syllabus related to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: There is no explicit or inferable content in the course syllabus related to health, well-being, or medical issues.

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly aims to cultivate 'operational skills,' 'problem-solving skills,' and 'expertise,' directly aligning with Target 4.4 (skills for employment and entrepreneurship) and indirectly with Target 4.7 (knowledge for sustainable development).
evidence: 問題解決能力(Students will acquire problem solving skills.)
奠定相關基礎專業知識(Students will acquire and apply expertise in their respective fields.)

Gender Equality.reason: The inference from 'social responsibility' to gender equality is too broad without specific evidence in the course text. There is no explicit mention of gender or related issues.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no explicit or inferable content in the course syllabus related to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: There is no explicit or inferable content in the course syllabus related to energy, renewables, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly focuses on 'innovation,' 'strategy planning,' and 'business model innovation,' directly supporting economic productivity, entrepreneurship, and decent job creation (Targets 8.2, 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course title 'Design Thinking and Innovation' and topics like '如何激發與管理創意？' directly align with Target 9.5, which emphasizes enhancing scientific research, technological capabilities, and innovation.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The 'empathize' phase of design thinking, focusing on understanding diverse user needs, can indirectly contribute to promoting social inclusion and reducing inequalities (Target 10.2).
evidence: 設計思考 發揮同理心
如何站在使用者的角度來瞭解他們潛在的需求

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: There is no explicit or inferable content in the course syllabus related to urban planning, sustainable cities, or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course's core competence in 'sustainability issues' combined with 'business model innovation' suggests an indirect link to developing sustainable consumption and production practices (Targets 12.2, 12.6).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' is a core competence, there is no specific mention of climate change, mitigation, or adaptation in the course syllabus.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no explicit or inferable content in the course syllabus related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or aquatic resources.

Life on Land.reason: There is no explicit or inferable content in the course syllabus related to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The 'empathize' phase and the application of design thinking in 'organizations' can indirectly foster more user-centric, inclusive, and effective institutional processes (Targets 16.6, 16.7).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly aims to develop 'teamwork' skills, which fosters collaboration and is a foundational element for effective partnerships (Target 17.17).
evidence: 團隊合作與領導能力(Students will be able to work effectively in teams as well as develop leadership skills.)
